description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rough, angular form and a predominantly gray and white coloration. The surface texture is coarse and uneven, with visible mineral grains and patches of lighter and darker tones. One side of the rock features a small, rectangular yellow label with black text, suggesting it has been cataloged or marked for identification. The rock's edges are jagged, and its faces vary from relatively flat to sharply inclined, giving it a rugged appearance. The coloration transitions from a lighter, almost white side to a darker, more weathered gray side, with some areas showing a tan or brownish hue. The overall impression is of a dense, solid stone that could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban environment.
description: A rough, angular gray and white rock with a coarse texture and a yellow identification label on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the rock, dominated by mottled gray and white coloration and a rugged, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rock's angular edge and the yellow label affixed near the top, with more pronounced white and gray mineral patterns.
description_view_2: The side with the yellow label is most visible here, showing a tan-brownish area and a flatter surface compared to other views.
description_view_3: This view reveals the darkest face of the rock, with deep gray tones and a more shadowed, jagged appearance.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
